ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sentiment_scores" (
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment score entry */,
  "text_id" INTEGER /* ID of the text that was analyzed */,
  "score_tag" INTEGER /* Tag indicating the overall sentiment (e.g., NONE, P, NEU, N) */,
  "agreement" TEXT /* Level of agreement (e.g., AGREEMENT, DISAGREEMENT) */,
  "subjectivity" TEXT /* Subjectivity of the text (e.g., OBJECTIVE, SUBJECTIVE) */,
  "confidence" INTEGER /* Confidence level of the analysis (0-100) */,
  "irony" TEXT /* Indicates if the sentiment is ironic or non-ironic */,
  "anger" REAL /* Score indicating the level of anger expressed */,
  "joy" REAL /* Score indicating the level of joy expressed */,
  "fear" REAL /* Score indicating the level of fear expressed */,
  "sadness" REAL /* Score indicating the level of sadness expressed */,
  "surprise" REAL /* Score indicating the level of surprise expressed */,
  "no_alphanumerics" INTEGER /* Count of non-alphanumeric characters in the text */,
  "negation_words" INTEGER /* Number of negation words in the text */,
  "word_number" INTEGER /* Total number of words in the text */,
  "misspelled" INTEGER /* Count of misspelled words */,
  "class" INTEGER /* Classification of the text (e.g., 1 for positive, 0 for negative) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment score was generated */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment score was last updated */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the machine learning model used for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_scores_text_id FOREIGN KEY ("text_id") REFERENCES texts ("text_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "texts" (
  "text_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each text entry */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the text (if applicable) */,
  "original_text" TEXT /* The original text to be analyzed for sentiment */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the text (e.g., social media, customer feedback) */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the text */,
  "submitted_date" TEXT /* Date when the text was submitted for analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("text_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., customer, admin) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysis_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each request for analysis */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the request */,
  "text_id" INTEGER /* ID of the text that was analyzed */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis request was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the analysis request (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "completed_at" TEXT /* Date when the analysis request was completed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_requests_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analysis_requests_text_id FOREIGN KEY ("text_id") REFERENCES texts ("text_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "emotional_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each emotional indicator entry */,
  "score_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated sentiment score */,
  "anger_score" REAL /* Score indicating the level of anger expressed */,
  "joy_score" REAL /* Score indicating the level of joy expressed */,
  "fear_score" REAL /* Score indicating the level of fear expressed */,
  "sadness_score" REAL /* Score indicating the level of sadness expressed */,
  "surprise_score" REAL /* Score indicating the level of surprise expressed */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the emotional indicator was generated */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the emotional indicator was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_emotional_indicators_score_id FOREIGN KEY ("score_id") REFERENCES sentiment_scores ("score_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "negation_words" (
  "negation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each negation word entry */,
  "text_id" INTEGER /* ID of the text that contains the negation word */,
  "word" TEXT /* The negation word itself */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the negation word was identified */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the negation word was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("negation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_negation_words_text_id FOREIGN KEY ("text_id") REFERENCES texts ("text_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "misspelled_words" (
  "misspelled_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each misspelled word entry */,
  "text_id" INTEGER /* ID of the text that contains the misspelled word */,
  "word" TEXT /* The misspelled word itself */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the misspelled word was identified */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the misspelled word was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("misspelled_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_misspelled_words_text_id FOREIGN KEY ("text_id") REFERENCES texts ("text_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment tag */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment tag */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "machine_learning_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each machine learning model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the machine learning model */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the machine learning model */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the model was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the model was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)
